PowerMenu.title: Power Menu
PowerMenu.description:
Anything to do with generating and distributing power on your ship can be found in the [color=PURPLE]Power[/color] menu.

Many items require power to operate, which can be supplied by a [color=KHAKI]Power Generator[/color].
Ensure each item is connected to the generator via [color=KHAKI]Power Cables[/color].

A [color=RED]red[/color] [color=KHAKI]Power Cable[/color] indicates the connection to each machine, so ensure all [color=RED]red[/color] [color=KHAKI]Power Cables[/color] are linked together.

AtmosphereMenu.title: Atmosphere Menu
AtmosphereMenu.description:
The [color=PURPLE]Atmosphere[/color] menu contains all the items you need to create and maintain a breathable atmosphere on your ship.

Your ship must be airtight and maintain the correct atmosphere.

Remember, any breaches, including open doors, will cause atmosphere to leak.

Many building types listed here will require [color=KHAKI]Atmospheric Ducting[/color] to be attached. This can be done by placing [color=KHAKI]Atmospheric Ducting[/color]  between the [color=RED]red[/color] termination points on the buildings.

ShipSystemsMenu.title: Ship Systems Menu
ShipSystemsMenu.description:
The [color=PURPLE]Ship Systems[/color] menu helps you see at a glance if your ship is operating smoothly.

You can view various information about your ship, and change its registered name, which is used to identify yourself throughout the [i]Slog[/i].

Pressing [color=PURPLE]Black Alert[/color] will order all crew back to the ship and remain there until the alert is deactivated.

Your [color=PURPLE]Burn Meter[/color] is also located on this menu.

CrewMenu.title: Crew Menu
CrewMenu.description:
All of your ship's crew, along with a quick overview of each, are listed in the [color=PURPLE]Crew[/color] menu.

Here you can view and assign [color=KHAKI]roles[/color], create custom [color=KHAKI]roles[/color], and assign hotkeys for those [color=KHAKI]roles[/color]. You can also set [color=KHAKI]schedules[/color] and [color=KHAKI]jobs[/color].

Selecting a crew member will open their individual menu, where you can view more detailed information about them.

Schedules.title: Schedules
Schedules.description:
You may want to set [color=KHAKI]schedules[/color] for each crew member to ensure they sleep and work at appropriate times. This can be achieved by accessing the [color=PURPLE]Schedules[/color] panel within the [color=PURPLE]Crew[/color] menu. You can then select the desired [color=PURPLE]Schedule[/color] button and fill in the time slots on the schedule grid.

Your crew will attempt to follow their assigned [color=KHAKI]schedule[/color] but will prioritise self-preservation tasks such as eating, drinking, and sleeping when necessary.

Jobs.title: Jobs
Jobs.description:
You may want to give each crew member specific job priorities to ensure they attempt those tasks first. This can be achieved by accessing the [color=PURPLE]Jobs[/color] panel within the [color=PURPLE]Crew[/color] menu.

[color=LIGHT_GREEN]Click[/color] each box to adjust the priority. Hovering over the icon above will show which jobs correspond to each priority.

A lower number indicates a higher priority.

Stockpiles.title: Stockpiles
Stockpiles.description:
You can adjust the priority of each [color=KHAKI]stockpile[/color] within the [color=PURPLE]Stockpile[/color] menu itself. This tells your crew which [color=KHAKI]stockpile[/color] should be filled first.

You can also set restrictions on what items can be stored within each [color=KHAKI]stockpile[/color] by selecting the desired categories in the [color=PURPLE]Stockpile[/color] menu.
